items: Multi-turn document generation apps should maintain session state storing both the semantic outline and generated file paths to enable iterative refinement.
Split FastAPI sub-apps into config/models/prompts/service/main modules when logic exceeds 150 lines or crosses distinct concern boundaries.
Separate LLM content generation (outlines) from document rendering (python-pptx) to allow text-level modifications before final file generation.
Provide downloadable FileResponse endpoints for generated assets, with storage paths isolated per session.
Support iterative refinement workflows where users pivot from initial generation to specific adjustments via chat commands.
Implement theme/configuration systems that constrain visual choices while allowing runtime customization.
When extending chat apps to support file uploads, audit existing utilities first—dedicated file parsers are often already implemented but not wired to the API.
Integrate file uploads into existing text flows by extracting content via utility modules and prepending to the user prompt.
Normalize multi-modal inputs into unified text content at the API entry point to avoid refactoring downstream logic.
Use consistent function naming between imports and definitions; Python NameError exceptions pinpoint exactly which function is missing from the module.
Lazy loading patterns (e.g., _ensure_pptx()) gracefully handle optional dependencies without breaking app initialization.
